mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, lowering the cost of capital and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable industrial production indicate robust aggregate demand without supply bottlenecks, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and flat or rising INDPRO fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, aggressive monetary tightening suppresses equity valuations, but if real GDP growth remains positive, the market prices in a soft landing, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 320 and GDPC1 is positive, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal savings decline below 700,000 million dollars, consumer deleveraging or reduced precautionary buffers signal spending contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS drops below 700,000 million dollars.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates a shift from residential to commercial investment, which historically precedes equity outperformance over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and HOUST momentum fails to predict positive 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, a rapid decline in the unemployment rate overrides rate-hike headwinds by confirming a labor-driven earnings boom, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% and UNRATE declines, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.
